title: cornus kousa subsp. chinensis
intro: Cornus kousa subsp. chinensis, also known as the Chinese dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its showy white or pink flowers and red berries. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cornus kousa subsp. chinensis are numerous, including its potential to reduce blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and neuroprotective effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is prepared into various forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
